auth_notes: Requires an API Token generated via the Samsara Dashboard (Settings > API Tokens). The token must be included in the HTTP header as 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>'.
rate_limits: Standard limit is 100 requests per second (RPS) per organization, with some specific endpoints having lower burst limits.
gotchas: Pagination uses a cursor-based system (after/before) which requires manual loop logic in n8n.
API tokens are scoped; if a token is created with insufficient permissions, requests will return 403 Forbidden without specific error details.
The 'all-time' data retrieval is not supported; most endpoints require specific start/end time parameters for historical queries.
client_must_provide: Specific API Scopes (Read/Write for Fleet, Industrial, or Drivers)
research_notes: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node. Users should configure a 'Header Auth' credential in n8n for the Bearer token. Webhooks must be registered via the API or the Samsara dashboard to point to an n8n Webhook node URL. Data is returned in clean JSON, making it easy to parse once the connection is established.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node. Users should configure a 'Header Auth' credential in n8n for the Bearer token. Webhooks must be registered via the API or the Samsara dashboard to point to an n8n Webhook node URL. Data is returned in clean JSON, making it easy to parse once the connection is established.
